summary: This source provides a brief, high-level definition of the Socratic method (also known as elenchus or the maieutic method). It describes the method as an argumentative dialogue relying on questions and clarifications to test a partner's reasoning, but does not contain direct quotes or detailed writings from Socrates himself.

frameworks.name: Socratic Method (Elenchus)
frameworks.when_to_apply: When engaging a conversation partner to examine their reasoning on a specific topic or philosophical issue.
steps: Probe the conversation partner on a topic using questions and clarifications.
Continue the dialogue until the partner is pressed to come to a conclusion on their own, or until their reasoning breaks down and they are forced to admit ignorance.
frameworks.source_quote: a form of argumentative dialogue in which an individual probes a conversation partner on a topic, using questions and clarifications, until the partner is pressed to come to a conclusion on their own, or else their reasoning breaks down and they are forced to admit ignorance.
